item.title: About
item.description: Hi! I’m Amy (she/her). I’m a first-year graduate student passionate about leveraging data and technology to advance social justice. I focus on applying technical skills to domestic social policy issues, including criminal justice and affordable housing, and aspire to work in civic tech. Having had the privilege of living in four major regions of the United States (I grew up in California, went to college in Texas, worked in New York, and now live and study in Illinois), I’m particularly drawn to local policy work and urban issues.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
I’m always excited to connect with people interested in social policy, civic tech, or anything else that caught your eye on this site.

If you have questions, would like to collaborate, or just want to say hello, please feel free to reach out! You can email me at amyslin at uchicago.edu.

You can also find me on LinkedIn or check out my code on GitHub.

item.title: Projects
item.description:
At the University of Chicago, I have developed my technical skillset through both coursework and extracurricular projects.

Course Projects

Projects where I collaborated with classmates to analyze social issues.

Project All Access Livable Housing: Housing and Homelessness in San Francisco

Tools: Python (Pandas, Plotly Dash, Altair)
Links: GitHub Repo
Course: Computer Science with Applications II

The Overview Working in a team of four, we built an end-to-end data application to analyze housing homelessness trends in San Francisco. The project is powered by a Python pipeline that ingests and standardizes disparate public datasets and ultimately produces a multi-dimensional analysis of relevant indicators with a dynamic choropleth map and line graphs visualizing covariates such as eviction rates, rent levels, encampment counts, and 311 service calls.

item.title: Publications
item.description:
In my most recent role at United Hospital Fund, I authored two data-driven publications exploring the intersection of health and social issues.

Click on the images below to read the publications.

Climate Change and Health: Understanding the Ripple Effects on Communities and Care
Sole Author

The Ripple Effects of the Adolescent Behavioral Health Crisis: Recent Trends and Impacts on American Adolescents, Families, and Society
Co-Author
